events.title: Trump issues stern Iran warning as Tehran angrily reacts to speech amid muted world reaction
events.description: Trump warns Iran in State of the Union: "I will never allow the world's number one sponsor of terror to have a nuclear weapon" while signaling diplomacy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump issues stern Iran warning as Tehran angrily reacts to speech amid muted world reaction

events.title: Death toll in Brazilian floods rises to 46 as rescuers continue search
events.description: Emergency workers continue to search for missing residents of towns reeling from recent floods.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Death toll in Brazilian floods rises to 46 as rescuers continue search

events.title: Cuba says 4 killed in speedboat shooting were attempting to infiltrate the country
events.description: Cuba says the 10 passengers on a boat that opened fire on its soldiers were armed Cubans living in the U.S. who were trying to infiltrate the island and unleash terrorism. Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the U.S. is gathering its own information.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Cuba says 4 killed in speedboat shooting were attempting to infiltrate the country

events.title: Mexico will make 'a strong show of security' ahead of World Cup, expert says
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Nathan P. Jones, Associate Professor of Security Studies in the College of Criminal Justice at Sam Houston State University, says that "the Mexican government will be making a strong show of security as a matter of national pride" ahead of the World Cup in the aftermath of the killing of drug lord El Mencho, whose death sparked several days of violence across the country.

events.title: Doing what’s right is ‘not a spectator sport’, Human Rights Council warned
events.description: In Geneva, delegates from more than 120 countries gathered on Monday to mark 20 years of the UN Human Rights Council and a shared commitment to international law, amid runaway global instability, wars and resurgent conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Doing what’s right is ‘not a spectator sport’, Human Rights Council warned

events.title: Floods ravage Minas Gerais, killing 36 as rescuers race to find dozens missing
events.description: Floods and landslides in Brazil’s Minas Gerais state have killed at least 36 people, and dozens remain missing
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Floods ravage Minas Gerais, killing 36 as rescuers race to find dozens missing

events.title: World News in Brief: UN humanitarian chief visits South Sudan, shelter fire risks in Gaza, West Bank violence
events.description: The UN Emergency Relief Coordinator arrived in South Sudan on Friday to visit one of the most under-reported humanitarian crises in the world, as clashes between government and opposition forces continue in Jonglei state.

events.title: Cuba says it killed heavily armed exiles who attacked from US-registered speedboat
events.description: Rare clash off island’s coast took place amid US oil embargo and heightened tensions between two countries Cuban forces killed four exiles and wounded six others who sailed into its waters onboard a Florida-registered speedboat and opened fire on a Cuban patrol, the country’s government said, at a time of heightened tensions with the US . Cuba’s interior ministry said the group was comprised of anti-government Cubans, some of whom were previously wanted for plotting attacks. They came from the US dressed in camouflage and armed with assault rifles, handguns, homemade explosives, ballistic vests and telescopic sights, it said. Continue reading...

events.title: After El Mencho's death, what lies ahead for Mexico’s Jalisco New Generation Cartel?
events.description: The killing of Nemesio "El Mencho" Oseguera Cervantes, leader of the powerful Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), marked a turning point in Mexico’s long war against organised crime. But after his death triggered unrest across the country this week, fears are mounting that internal rivalries and territorial battles could trigger more violence.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 After El Mencho's death, what lies ahead for Mexico’s Jalisco New Generation Cartel?

events.title: US: Rubio and Vance warn of Iran's threat ahead of talks
events.description: US and Iranian negotiators are set to meet for nuclear talks in Geneva on Thursday, with senior figures in Washington warning that Tehran could one day pose a military threat to the continental United States.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US: Rubio and Vance warn of Iran's threat ahead of talks

events.title: World News in Brief: Civilian casualties in Afghanistan, migrant deaths off Crete, call for justice in Brazil trial
events.description: The UN Assistance Mission in Afghanistan (UNAMA) has received credible reports of civilian casualties following airstrikes carried out by Pakistan inside Afghanistan late on 21 February and into the early hours of the next day.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Civilian casualties in Afghanistan, migrant deaths off Crete, call for justice in Brazil trial

events.title: Cuban coast guard kills 4 in exchange of gunfire with stolen speedboat as ministry IDs 'criminal' suspects
events.description: Four were killed and six were wounded in a shooting in Cuban territorial waters involving a U.S. speedboat as Secretary of State Marco Rubio attends a Caribbean summit.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Cuban coast guard kills 4 in exchange of gunfire with stolen speedboat as ministry IDs 'criminal' suspects

events.title: Cuba coast guard kills 4 on Florida-registered boat
events.description: Cuba’s government said Wednesday that its soldiers killed four people aboard a speed boat registered in Florida that opened fire on officers in Cuban waters. Cuba’s Interior Ministry issued a statement that provided few details about the shooting, but noted that the boat was roughly 1.6 km northeast of Cayo Falcones, off Cuba’s north coast. It was unclear if any U.S. citizens were aboard. FRANCE 24's Ed Augustin reports from Havana.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Cuba coast guard kills 4 on Florida-registered boat

events.title: Cuba says it shot dead four people on US-registered speedboat
events.description: The passengers were Cuban nationals living in the US and intended to "carry out an infiltration for terrorist purposes", the Cuban government says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Cuba says it shot dead four people on US-registered speedboat

events.title: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un warns South Korea, says US should end hostility

events.title: ICC judges assess case against Duterte over Philippines ‘war on drugs’ killings
events.description: Judges at the UN-backed International Criminal Court (ICC) on Monday opened a confirmation of charges hearing for former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, to determine whether there is sufficient evidence to send him to trial on alleged crimes against humanity linked to killings during the country’s so-called “war on drugs”.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ICC judges assess case against Duterte over Philippines ‘war on drugs’ killings

events.title: Troops reinforce Puerto Vallarta as unrest shows signs of easing following El Mencho’s death
events.description: Mexico's Navy deploys 103 Marines and tactical vehicles to Jalisco after cartel leader "El Mencho" killed, as authorities brace for prolonged violence and power struggles.

events.title: Floods and landslides in Brazil kill at least 30 after record rainfall
events.description: Firefighters search for 39 people missing in debris after river burst and houses were swept away Three firefighters pulled a man’s body from the mud amid the rubble of houses swept away in a landslide in south-eastern Brazil, where 30 people died and 39 were still missing on Tuesday after torrential rains. A river in the state of Minas Gerais burst its banks and streets became raging currents of brown water after an overnight downpour in a region that has seen record rain this month. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Floods and landslides in Brazil kill at least 30 after record rainfall

events.title: Over a billion fear losing land and homes within five years
events.description: Despite global progress in strengthening land tenure and governance, more than a billion people worldwide – nearly one in four adults – fear they could lose the rights to some or all of their land and housing within the next five years.

events.title: Peru declares a state of emergency for hundreds of districts after severe rains and flooding
events.description: Peru has declared a state of emergency across more than 700 districts after heavy rain triggered floods and landslides linked to El Nino Costero
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Peru declares a state of emergency for hundreds of districts after severe rains and flooding

events.title: Sen Mullin urges spring breakers to cancel trips to Mexico amid country's violence: 'No one should be going'
events.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin urged spring breakers to cancel Mexico trips after cartel leader "El Mencho" was killed, sparking violent clashes and unrest in the region.

events.title: Four killed in firefight between Cuban coastguard and Florida-registered speedboat
events.description: A confrontation in Cuban waters left four people dead on Wednesday after soldiers opened fire on a Florida-registered speedboat that Havana says attacked first. Washington is investigating whether any victims were US citizens, as the incident threatens to deepen already strained relations between the two countries.

events.title: ‘A devastating force’: how recent Mediterranean storms turned to tragedies
events.description: Atmospheric machine-gun has fired storm after deadly storm at the region this year, leaving a trail of widespread destruction For Andrés Sánchez Barea, in Spain, it was the fear that arose when water started to spurt from plug sockets. For Nelson Duarte, in Portugal, it was the helplessness that hit as violent winds smacked down trees and tore tiles from roofs. For Amal Essuide, in Morocco, it was the reality that dawned when a corpse was pulled onboard a boat in the flooded medina. Each moment of horror is a fragment of the destruction wrought by an atmospheric machine-gun that in recent weeks has fired storm after storm at the western Mediterranean. Scientists do not know if climate breakdown helped pull the trigger, but research suggests it loaded the chamber with bigger bullets. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘A devastating force’: how recent Mediterranean storms turned to tragedies

events.title: World Central Kitchen calls international community not to forget Kyiv and Gaza
events.description: Today in Spotlight, we speak with celebrity Chef José Andrés. The founder of World Food Kitchen talks of his Longer Tables Fund's matching donations to support critical energy infrastructure in Ukraine, Gaza where his charity provides one million hot meals a day... and more.

events.title: South Sudan fighting displaces nearly 280,000; UN warns of ‘perfect storm’
events.description: Renewed fighting in South Sudan’s Jonglei state has displaced hundreds of thousands of civilians in recent weeks, damaged health facilities, fuelled the spread of cholera and prompting the UN relief chief to warn of a “perfect storm” of conflict, climate shocks and deprivation.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ South Sudan fighting displaces nearly 280,000; UN warns of ‘perfect storm’

events.title: U.S. and Iran to hold a third round of nuclear talks in Geneva
events.description: Iran and the United States prepared to meet Thursday in Geneva for nuclear negotiations, as America has gathered a fleet of aircraft and warships to the Middle East to pressure Tehran into a deal.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. and Iran to hold a third round of nuclear talks in Geneva

events.title: FIFA's Infantino confident Mexico can co-host World Cup despite cartel violence
events.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino says he has "complete confidence" in Mexico as a World Cup co-host despite days of cartel violence in the country that has left at least 70 people dead.

events.title: World Cup fans visiting Mexico face 'no risk' after cartel violence, says president
events.description: Mexico's president has insisted there is "no risk" to fans coming to the country for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, after the killing of a cartel boss sparked days of violence.

events.title: Ukraine: ‘Use every diplomatic tool to end this war’, top UN official tells Security Council

events.title: Berlinale executives summoned over film director's anti-Israel speech
events.description: German culture officials plan to summon organisers of the Berlin film festival Thursday after the event was rocked this year by controversy over the Israel-Palestinian conflict and Gaza war. Bild daily reported that Tricia Tuttle, the US director of the Berlinale, is expected to be sacked at the meeting, citing sources close to the KBB, the state-owned company that runs the festival. FRANCE 24's Nick Holdsworth reports from Berlin.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Berlinale executives summoned over film director's anti-Israel speech

events.title: Online disinformation fueled panic after the killing of Mexico's most powerful drug lord

events.title: US accused of ‘shameless exploitation’ over proposed Zambian health aid deal
events.description: Leaked draft of $1bn memorandum of understanding reveals mandatory targets, sharing of data, and reported access to mining concessions The US has been accused of “shameless exploitation” over a health financing agreement with Zambia worth more than $1bn (£740m), amid warnings that the country is getting a raw deal from the Trump administration. A leaked draft of a five-year memorandum of understanding (MOU) between the two countries, seen by the Guardian, reveals that Zambia may accept terms worse than health financing agreements the US has reached with 16 other African countries . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US accused of ‘shameless exploitation’ over proposed Zambian health aid deal

events.title: Fear over potential US strike on Iran puts Lebanon on edge

events.title: Americans recount chaos as Mexico unrest subsides after cartel boss death

events.title: Minns government undermined own goal with NSW protest restrictions, constitutional challenge hears
events.description: Lawyer for protest groups argues state needs to prove it was ‘rational to prevent all protests’ to achieve social cohesion Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Lawyers for three protest groups have argued a Minns government law which effectively banned marches in Sydney’s CBD made its own objective of enhancing social cohesion “worse”. On Thursday the NSW court of appeal heard the constitutional challenge against the anti-protest law, which was passed in the wake of the Bondi terror. Continue reading...

events.title: South Sudan: Funding gaps threaten nearly 2 million displaced people

events.title: Live: Russia slams Ukraine with missiles and drones ahead of US-Ukraine talks

His remarks come amid speculation over a possible meeting with US President Donald Trump.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: North Korea's Kim pledges economic push as his sister is elevated at party congress
events.description: Members of North Korea's ruling Workers' Party have been gathering in Pyongyang this week, where Kim Jong Un is pledging to boost the economy over the next fives years. While there's been no sign of his daughter, who's emerged as Kim's heir apparent, the secretive leader has promoted his sister to the equivalent of a cabinet position. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Ramon Pacheco Pardo, Professor of International Relations at King’s College London.

events.title: Robert Mugabe’s son charged with attempted murder over Johannesburg shooting
events.description: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, known for lavish lifestyle, also accused of theft and being illegal immigrant after man allegedly shot in back A son of the late Zimbabwean president Robert Mugabe has been charged with attempted murder after a 23-year-old man was allegedly shot in the back on 19 February in an upmarket area of Johannesburg. Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe, 28, appeared in court on Monday for a brief hearing alongside co-accused Tobias Mugabe Matonhodze. Mugabe’s lawyer Sinenhlanhla Mnguni declined to comment when asked by reporters whether the two men were related. Mnguni said he would request bail for his clients at the next hearing on 3 March. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Robert Mugabe’s son charged with attempted murder over Johannesburg shooting

events.title: South Korea’s birthrate rises for second year with experts saying ‘echo boomers’ behind boost
events.description: Rebound in the country – which has been having demographic crisis – said to be partly because of 3.6 million born between 1991 and 1995 having children South Korea recorded 254,500 births in 2025, the largest annual increase in 15 years, driven largely by a temporarily enlarged generation – known as “echo boomers” – now in their early thirties, alongside marriage rates recovering from Covid-era delays. The country’s fertility rate – the average number of babies a woman is expected to have in her lifetime – rose to 0.80 from 0.75 last year , returning to the 0.8 range for the first time since 2021, according to provisional figures released by South Korea’s ministry of data and statistics on Wednesday. Continue reading...

events.title: Germany news: Passenger attacks conductor on ICE train
events.description: A female passenger punched the inspector on a long-distance train to Berlin during a ticket check. Meanwhile, renovating the federal president's official residence would cost an estimate of €600 million. Follow DW.

events.title: Myriad fragments, one tragedy: How four years of war changed Ukraine

events.title: Caribbean leaders call for ‘de-escalation and dialogue’ amid US oil embargo on Cuba

During the opening ceremony of the four-day Caricom summit in St Kitts and Nevis, leaders of the regional bloc called for a strategic collaborations to deal with the impact of recent US policies. events.title: Democrats call missing Trump documents in Epstein files ‘largest cover-up in modern history’
events.description: Documents related to a woman’s sexual assault complaint against Donald Trump in the Jeffrey Epstein files were not released by the FBI, according to investigative reports. Democrats on the House Oversight Committee called it the "largest government cover-up in modern history". The US Justice Department said it’s reviewing files to see if any were handled "improperly" but denied wrongdoing.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Democrats call missing Trump documents in Epstein files ‘largest cover-up in modern history’

events.title: Justice Department accused of withholding and removing some Epstein files related to Trump
events.description: This Wednesday it has emerged that there could be missing information in the Epstein Files, in particular relating to a woman who accused Donald Trump of sexual assault decades ago. Democrat lawmaker Robert Garcia has flagged up the missing links. Garcia is on the House Oversight committee. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum reports.

events.title: LIVE: UN marks fourth anniversary of Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine
events.description: Four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the UN is marking the day with high-level debate and renewed calls to end the war - including in the General Assembly which passed a resolution reaffirming its strong commitment to the sovereignty, independence and territorial integrity of Ukraine. The UN chief just told the Security Council diplomats must use every tool available to finally end the war. UN News App users can follow the coverage here. For full UN meetings coverage today and every day, go here.

events.title: RSF siege of El Fasher in Sudan has ‘hallmarks of genocide’, UN mission finds
events.description: Report details harrowing 18-month occupation of North Darfur capital, showing destruction aimed at ethnic communities The siege and capture of the Sudanese city of El Fasher by the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces group last October bore “the hallmarks of genocide”, a UN-mandated fact-finding mission has said. In a report detailing the harrowing 18-month occupation of the capital of North Darfur, investigators concluded that the RSF and allied militias deliberately inflicted conditions calculated to bring about the physical destruction of the Zaghawa and Fur ethnic communities. Continue reading...

Tommy Schaefer was sentenced to 18 years in prison in Bali for the 2014 murder of Sheila von Wiese-Mack, the mother of Heather Mack, during a luxury holiday, in a case that became known as the Bali suitcase murder. Prosecutors allege the couple were trying to gain access to a $1.5m (£1.1m) trust fund. Continue reading...

events.title: Brazilian politician brothers convicted of ordering murder of Rio city councillor
events.description: João Francisco Inácio Brazão and Domingos Inácio Brazão sentenced for murder of Marielle Franco, a gay Black woman and rising political star Two influential Brazilian politician brothers have been convicted by Brazil’s supreme court of ordering the murder of Marielle Franco , the Rio de Janeiro city councillor, nearly eight years ago. João Francisco Inácio Brazão, the former congressman known as Chiquinho, and the former adviser to Rio’s court of auditors Domingos Inácio Brazão were sentenced to 76 years and three months in prison for the murders of Franco, 38, and her driver, Anderson Gomes, 39. events.title: Anger over plans to abolish New Zealand’s dedicated environment ministry

Under the plan, the department would be folded into a new “mega-ministry” that will cover housing, urban development, transport, local government and the environment. Continue reading...

events.title: Brazil politicians convicted for ordering murder of black activist councilor

events.description: This blog is now closed Grace Tame says ‘spare me the condescension, old man’ after Albanese defends ‘difficult’ comment Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Advocate for gambling reform reiterates calls for regulator and ban on ads Reverend Tim Costello , the chief advocate for the Alliance for Gambling Reform, spoke to RN Breakfast this morning about efforts to combat gambling, including the rollout of BetStop, a self-exclusion register . We are literally saturated. You know, sadly, gambling companies now even own our kids … Right at the moment, you have the farcical situation that under 16, you can’t be on social media, which I support. But they’re inundated with gambling ads online, on TV. 900,000 of young Australians gambled last year, even though it’s illegal now. Continue reading...

events.title: Grace Tame says ‘spare me the condescension, old man’ after Albanese defends ‘difficult’ comment
events.description: PM clarifies remark but says he disagrees with some language the 2021 Australian of the Year has used Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former Australian of the Year Grace Tame has said “we all know what you meant” after the Australian prime minister, Anthony Albanese, claimed he meant to describe her “difficult life” when he labelled her “difficult”. Speaking in Melbourne on Thursday morning, the prime minister said he had not meant to describe Tame as “difficult” at a News Corp event on Wednesday, when he was asked to describe public figures in one word. Instead he meant that her life had been “difficult”, he said. Continue reading...

events.title: M 7.1 - 55 km NNW of Kota Belud, Malaysia
events.description: PAGER - GREEN ShakeMap - V DYFI? - III Time 2026-02-22 16:57:46 UTC 2026-02-22 16:57:46 UTC at epicenter Location 6.829°N 116.264°E Depth 619.85 km (385.16 mi)

events.title: China restricts exports to 40 Japanese entities with ties to military
events.description: China on Tuesday restricted exports to 40 Japanese entities it says are contributing to Japan's "remilitarization," in the latest escalation of tensions with Tokyo.

events.title: The Louvre Museum's director has resigned in the wake of October's brazen jewel heist
events.description: French President Emmanuel Macron accepted Laurence des Cars' resignation as "an act of responsibility" at a moment when the Louvre needs security upgrades, modernization and other major projects.

events.title: Modi: India stands with Israel 'with full conviction'
events.description: Indian Prime Minister Modi is on a two-day visit to Israel and gave a speech at the Knesset. Modi hailed the relationship between the two countries, with Prime Minister Netanyahu calling him a "brother."

events.title: Nickolay Mladenov: the Bulgarian leading the Board of Peace
events.description: As director-general of Donald Trump's Board of Peace and High Representative for Gaza, Bulgarian Nickolay Mladenov faces a massive challenge. Who is the man tasked with implementing the US-brokered peace plan for Gaza?

events.title: Chagos Islands deal sows confusion over UK minister remarks
events.description: The BBC said a government source has denied that the UK Parliament has halted a ratification process to turn over the control of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius. It comes after a UK minister said the process was paused.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Chagos Islands deal sows confusion over UK minister remarks

events.title: Paris Saint-Germain edge Monaco to reach Champions League last 16
events.description: Paris Saint-Germain survived a tense night against Monaco on Wednesday, drawing the second leg but advancing on aggregate after playing much of the match against 10 men. Late drama and a red card shifted momentum as the holders did just enough to secure their place in the Champions League last 16.

events.title: Former Brazil lawmakers convicted of ordering murder of Rio councilwoman Marielle Franco
events.description: Two former politicians were convicted by Brazil’s Supreme Court on Wednesday of ordering the 2018 assassination of Rio de Janeiro councilwoman and activist Marielle Franco, whose murder exposed a web of corruption linking political figures to organised crime.

events.title: 'I failed three or four exams': Meet the kids who want a social media ban
events.description: Is social media messing up our kids?

events.title: Pair charged with alleged murder of 'mistakenly-kidnapped' pensioner, 85
events.description: Two men have been charged with the alleged kidnap and murder of a pensioner in Sydney.

events.title: Mexican Congress approves trimming work week to 40 hours
events.description: Mexican lawmakers have approved President Claudia Sheinbaum’s initiative to gradually reduce the work week from 48 hours to 40 hours

events.title: Norwegian king will remain in Spanish hospital a few more days, doctor says
events.description: Norway’s king will remain in a hospital for a few more days for treatment and observation after being admitted during a vacation on the Spanish island of Tenerife

events.title: Cambodian opposition politician convicted of inciting social disorder gets early prison release
events.description: Cambodia has freed opposition politician Sun Chanthy early after King Norodom Sihamoni granted him a royal pardon

events.title: Mexico sends second aid package to Cuba as US tightens energy blockade
events.description: Mexico has sent a second humanitarian aid package to Cuba as the island struggles with fuel shortages and tighter U.S. pressure

events.title: Orbán orders extra security at energy sites, claiming Ukraine plots disruptions
events.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán ordered extra security at critical energy infrastructure sites after claiming Ukraine was attempting to disrupt Hungary’s energy system

events.title: ‘I dream of a safe life’: Gaza’s children share the future they want
events.description: Children in Gaza are voicing their demands for the future through a UN-run initiative that seeks to amplify their voices and restore the “fundamentals of childhood”.

events.title: Minnesota middle school teaches eighth graders ICE 'harassed' migrants in geography class curriculum
events.description: A Minnesota middle school taught an eighth grade geography lesson describing ICE as “harassing" migrants and assigned a video on Trump’s deportation efforts for extra credit.

events.title: Terrifying moment caught on camera as road collapse suddenly swallows vehicles at busy intersection
events.description: Dramatic video captured a road collapse swallowing two cars near the University of Nebraska-Omaha. Security footage showed the road behind the intersection giving way underground.

events.title: California sanctuary policies blamed after ICE arrests 9 sex offenders in Los Angeles
events.description: ICE arrests nine convicted sex offenders in Los Angeles as California accused of releasing thousands of criminal migrants instead of honoring detainers.

events.title: Child predator dubbed 'monster parents fear most' cleared for release through California parole program
events.description: A California child molester once called "the monster parents fear most" was cleared for release under an elderly parole program despite widespread community opposition.

events.title: Former Clinton, Obama official to resign from Harvard over Epstein ties
events.description: Former Harvard President Larry Summers announced that he will retire from his academic and faculty appointments at the Ivy League school, a spokesperson said.

events.title: Human remains found in submerged car believed to belong to Florida mother who vanished 22 years ago
events.description: A family may get answers in the the decades-long search for mother Mary Lou Combs after investigators recovered human remains from a Florida waterway.

events.title: College student found in snow-covered car dies of carbon monoxide poisoning during Rhode Island blizzard
events.description: College student dies of carbon monoxide poisoning after being found unconscious in snow-covered car during Rhode Island's historic blizzard.

events.title: Nancy Guthrie's neighbor saw suspicious man walking nearby 2 weeks before suspected abduction
events.description: A Tucson neighbor said she saw a suspicious man near Nancy Guthrie’s home weeks before her suspected abduction. Investigators are reviewing security footage as they refine the timeline.

events.title: US military base at Diego Garcia thrown back into uncertainty amid Chagos deal turmoil
events.description: U.K. Parliament ratification of Chagos Islands sovereignty transfer to Mauritius remains uncertain as House of Lords considers legislation amid transatlantic tensions.

events.title: Mexican president considers legal action against Elon Musk over drug cartel accusation
events.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum considers legal action against Elon Musk after he accused her of having cartel ties without providing evidence.

events.title: Former Norwegian PM Thorbjørn Jagland hospitalized amid Epstein probe
events.description: Former Norwegian PM Thorbjørn Jagland has been hospitalized due to strain from corruption charges linked to Jeffrey Epstein files, his attorney confirmed.

events.title: CIA urges Iranians to use burner phones, Tor to contact US in Persian-language video
events.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe joins classified Iran briefing as agency releases Farsi video providing digital security guidance for Iranian activists seeking to contact U.S. intelligence.
events.ticker_label: 💥 CIA urges Iranians to use burner phones, Tor to contact US in Persian-language video

events.title: Louvre Museum director resigns following $102M theft of crown jewels
events.description: Louvre director Laurence des Cars resigns after thieves stole $102 million in crown jewels. Macron praised her decision as "an act of responsibility."
